live_path.role: parses --owner and forwards the parsed value into the service entrypoint
request orchestrator that resolves the effective owner, persists the base record, derives routing_key, and assembles the emitted payload
derives effective_owner and owner_source from the CLI owner or the configured default owner
persists the base record using the resolved owner only
returns the stored/base record with name, status, and owner only
derives routing_key from the same resolved owner and request name
hands the stored record plus already-derived owner_source and routing_key to the serializer
emits the payload by copying the record and attaching owner_source and routing_key
JSON-serializes the emitted payload for CLI output

inputs: CLI --owner value when present and non-blank
config/defaults.json owner when --owner is omitted or blank
logic: returns [trimmed owner, explicit] when owner is provided

test_observations.observation: sync_item() falls back to config/defaults.json owner and sets owner_source='default' while routing_key uses that same default owner
CLI path preserves the explicit owner through to the emitted payload

test_observations.source: local CLI run: python -m sync_app.cli --name 'Launch Checklist' --status pending --owner pm-oncall
test_observations.observation: emitted JSON included owner_source='explicit' and routing_key='pm-oncall:launch-checklist'

test_observations.source: local CLI run: python -m sync_app.cli --name 'Launch Checklist' --status pending
test_observations.observation: emitted JSON included owner='pm-oncall', owner_source='default', and routing_key='pm-oncall:launch-checklist' from the configured default owner

rejected_decoys.claim: owner_source looks like it comes from storage and routing_key is probably computed before the CLI applies --owner
rejected_decoys.reason_rejected: live code derives owner_source in service._resolve_owner and computes routing_key in service.sync_item after the CLI has already passed owner into the service

rejected_decoys.claim: store helper likely became the source of truth after the serializer cleanup
rejected_decoys.reason_rejected: the file is explicitly an unfinished draft, and the live store helper make_record only returns name/status/owner

rejected_decoys.claim: storage may be the live source of owner_source
rejected_decoys.reason_rejected: sync_item does not call this legacy helper; it calls make_record instead
sync_item calls serializer.build_routing_key, not the legacy store helper

rejected_decoys.claim: owner attribution may move into serializer helpers
rejected_decoys.reason_rejected: the document is future-state design intent only and does not describe current live behavior
